hints.Quo: 'Quo' is an interrogative adverb meaning 'to what point'
hints.usque: 'usque' is an adverb meaning 'all the way / continuously'
hints.tandem: 'tandem' is an adverb meaning 'at last / finally'

hints.Present: 'abutere' is the 2nd person singular future of the deponent 'abuti'
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'abutebaris' — 'abutere' is future indicative
hints.Perfect: Perfect would be 'abusus es' — 'abutere' is a simple future form

hints.utor, uti, usus sum: These are the principal parts of 'uti' (to use) — the base verb without the ab- prefix
hints.loquor, loqui, locutus sum: These are the principal parts of 'loqui' (to speak), a different deponent verb
hints.sequor, sequi, secutus sum: These are the principal parts of 'sequi' (to follow), a different deponent verb

hints.Pronoun: 'Quo' can be a relative pronoun, but here with 'usque' it's an interrogative adverb
hints.Conjunction: 'Quo' is not joining clauses here — it asks a question
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns — 'Quo' here is interrogative
